description_long: This is a classic pitchfork with a long, straight cylindrical handle and four evenly spaced, pointed metal tines. The handle appears to be made of metal, giving it a uniform, dark finish throughout its length. The tines are robust and slightly tapered, designed for efficient penetration and lifting of materials such as hay, leaves, or debris. The transition from the handle to the tines is smooth, with no visible grip or ergonomic features at the top. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty use in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas. The surface is matte and hard, with no visible signs of rust or wear.
description: A dark, metal pitchfork with a straight handle and four pointed tines, designed for heavy-duty urban use. The surface is matte and robust, with a utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: The pitchfork is seen edge-on, showing only the thin profile of the handle and a single tine.
description_view_1: The pitchfork is viewed from the front, clearly displaying all four tines and the full width of the head.
description_view_2: The pitchfork is seen edge-on from the opposite side, again showing only the thin profile and a single tine.
description_view_3: The pitchfork is viewed from the back, revealing all four tines and the full head, similar to the front view.
